title: Singapore 10-Day City Break
metaTitle: Singapore 10-Day City Break | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 10-day trip to Singapore, Singapore: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Gardens by the Bay (Cloud Forest & Flower Dome) and Marina Bay Sands SkyPark Observation Deck. Prices from $2741 per person (estimate).
highlights: Gardens by the Bay (Cloud Forest & Flower Dome)
Marina Bay Sands SkyPark Observation Deck
Infinity pool stay at Marina Bay Sands
overview: Spend 10 days discovering Singapore, Singapore — Tropical rainforest climate that is hot and humid year-round (highs 30-32°C) with frequent short afternoon showers, especially during the November-January monsoon. February to April offers slightly lower humidity and rainfall, though Singapore's equatorial climate is warm and can see rain year-round, so any month works with proper planning. This itinerary blends Gardens by the Bay (Cloud Forest & Flower Dome), Marina Bay Sands SkyPark Observation Deck, Merlion Park with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
9 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Gardens by the Bay (Cloud Forest & Flower Dome), Marina Bay Sands SkyPark Observation Deck, Merlion Park
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Singapore
itinerary.description: Land at Singapore Changi Airport (SIN) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Gardens by the Bay (Cloud Forest & Flower Dome) — Futuristic domed conservatories and Supertree Grove. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.
activities: Gardens by the Bay (Cloud Forest & Flower Dome)

itinerary.title: Singapore: Marina Bay Sands SkyPark Observation Deck & Singapore Zoo
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Marina Bay Sands SkyPark Observation Deck — Panoramic city skyline views atop the iconic hotel. In the afternoon, continue to Singapore Zoo — Open-concept zoo rated among the world's best. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Marina Bay Sands SkyPark Observation Deck

itinerary.title: Singapore: Universal Studios Singapore & Night Safari
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Universal Studios Singapore — Movie-themed amusement park on Sentosa Island. In the afternoon, continue to Night Safari — World's first nocturnal wildlife park. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Sentosa Island
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Sentosa Island, a popular side trip from Singapore. Return to Singapore in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Tiong Bahru heritage neighborhood — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Singapore: Singapore Flyer & ArtScience Museum
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Singapore Flyer — Giant observation wheel with city and strait views. In the afternoon, continue to ArtScience Museum — Lotus-shaped museum blending art, science and technology. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Pulau Ubin
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Pulau Ubin, a popular side trip from Singapore. Return to Singapore in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Singapore: S.E.A. Aquarium & River Wonders
itinerary.description: Morning visit to S.E.A. Aquarium — One of the world's largest aquariums, on Sentosa. In the afternoon, continue to River Wonders — River-themed wildlife park with giant pandas. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Pulau Ubin rustic island — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Singapore Cable Car (Sentosa) or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to SIN for your departure flight.

travelTips: Eat at hawker centers for some of the best-value, highest-quality food in the city, including Michelin-starred stalls.
Buy an EZ-Link card immediately on arrival at Changi Airport for seamless transit.
Visit Gardens by the Bay at night for the free Supertree light show in addition to daytime conservatory visits.
Changi Airport itself is a destination — Jewel Changi's indoor waterfall is worth a visit even between flights.
Book Universal Studios and major attraction tickets online in advance for time savings and occasional discounts.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Light, breathable clothing for constant heat and humidity
A light jacket for heavily air-conditioned malls and the MRT
A compact umbrella for sudden tropical showers
Modest attire for temple and mosque visits
thingsToDo: Gardens by the Bay (Cloud Forest & Flower Dome)
Marina Bay Sands SkyPark Observation Deck
Gardens by the Bay outdoor gardens
Marina Bay Sands light and water show
thingsToAvoid: Chewing gum import/sale — it is restricted by law (personal use with no resale is generally tolerated, but don't bring large quantities).
Jaywalking, littering, or eating/drinking on the MRT — fines are strictly enforced.
Assuming tipping is expected — it is not customary and can occasionally cause confusion.
Overspending on Orchard Road malls when hawker centers and heartland neighborhoods offer better value and authenticity.
Underestimating the humidity — plan indoor/air-conditioned breaks between outdoor sightseeing.
topRestaurants: Hawker Chan (Liao Fan Soya Sauce Chicken) — Michelin Street Food ($)
Lau Pa Sat hawker center — Hawker / Various ($)
Maxwell Food Centre stalls — Hawker / Various ($)
instagramSpots: Gardens by the Bay Supertree Grove at night
Marina Bay Sands infinity pool skyline
hiddenGems: Haw Par Villa quirky statue park
familyActivities: Sentosa beaches and Adventure Cove Waterpark
adventureActivities: Skypark climb at Marina Bay Sands
luxuryExperiences: Infinity pool stay at Marina Bay Sands
Private yacht cruise around the Marina
VIP F1 Singapore Grand Prix experience
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset at Marina Bay Sands SkyPark
Private dinner amid the Gardens by the Bay Supertrees
Couples spa retreat at a luxury hotel
budgetExperiences: Hawker center meals for a few dollars
Free Gardens by the Bay outdoor areas and Supertree light show
Merlion Park and Marina Bay waterfront walk
Free entry to many Botanic Gardens sections
localEtiquette: Chewing gum sale/import is restricted, and littering, jaywalking and eating on the MRT carry real fines.
Tipping is not customary and generally not expected — a service charge is usually already included.
Remove shoes when entering someone's home.
Queueing is taken seriously and orderly lines are the social norm everywhere.
Dress modestly when visiting mosques, temples, and Little India's religious sites.
localTransportGuide: The MRT subway is clean, efficient and covers virtually the entire city — an EZ-Link or contactless card works across MRT, buses and even some taxis. Grab ride-hailing is widely used, and the city is very walkable in the downtown core with covered walkways in many areas.
bestTimeToVisit: February to April offers slightly lower humidity and rainfall, though Singapore's equatorial climate is warm and can see rain year-round, so any month works with proper planning.
weather: Tropical rainforest climate that is hot and humid year-round (highs 30-32°C) with frequent short afternoon showers, especially during the November-January monsoon.
visaInformation: Visa-free entry for most nationalities including the US, UK, Canada, Australia and EU citizens for tourist stays of up to 30-90 days depending on nationality. Verify current requirements against your specific passport before travel.
averageFlightTime: ~11.1h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Singapore?
faqs.answer: Visa-free entry for most nationalities including the US, UK, Canada, Australia and EU citizens for tourist stays of up to 30-90 days depending on nationality. Verify current requirements against your specific passport before travel.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Singapore?
faqs.answer: February to April offers slightly lower humidity and rainfall, though Singapore's equatorial climate is warm and can see rain year-round, so any month works with proper planning. Tropical rainforest climate that is hot and humid year-round (highs 30-32°C) with frequent short afternoon showers, especially during the November-January monsoon.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Singapore safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Singapore is rated very safe for tourists, with low rates of violent crime. Usual travel precautions (watch belongings in crowds, use licensed taxis) still apply.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Singapore?
faqs.answer: The local currency is SGD. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: It also works well for families — highlights include Universal Studios Singapore.

reviews.author: Noah Müller
reviews.title: Loved every moment
reviews.body: Our trip to Singapore was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Little India was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Daniel Ibrahim
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Night Safari and Singapore Flyer. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Ethan Cohen
reviews.title: Amazing itinerary, zero stress
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Singapore Flyer and Henderson Waves bridge. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Daniel Rossi
reviews.title: Consultant made it effortless
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Gardens by the Bay outdoor gardens and S.E.A. Aquarium. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Mia Fernandez
reviews.title: Best vacation we've had
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Kampong Glam (Arab Street) and Universal Studios Singapore. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: James Ivanov
reviews.title: So many hidden gems included
reviews.body: Our trip to Singapore was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Singapore Cable Car (Sentosa) was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Liam Larsen
reviews.title: Exceeded our expectations
reviews.body: Our trip to Singapore was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Singapore Zoo was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Daniel Williams
reviews.title: Perfect for our honeymoon
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to ArtScience Museum and Singapore Flyer. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Yuki Garcia
reviews.title: Unforgettable trip!
reviews.body: Marina Bay Sands SkyPark Observation Deck alone was worth the trip, but combined with Merlion Park this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Tom Santos
reviews.title: Great for families with kids
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond S.E.A. Aquarium and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Wei Wang
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: Booked this as a family of 4 trip and it was well paced. Marina Bay Sands light and water show in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Kampong Glam (Arab Street) and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.
